title: D-St set for a positive opening as GIFT Nifty signals firm start
summary: Domestic equities closed lower on Tuesday, with the Nifty declining 0.7 percent. Rising crude oil prices and fresh foreign investor outflows weighed on sentiment. Global markets also showed weakness, adding to market volatility during the derivatives expiry. Analysts anticipate sideways trading with some volatility due to geopolitical tensions. Investors will monitor key global macro data and earnings for stock-specific action.

title: Stocks in news: Groww, Delhivery, Infosys, Wipro, IDBI Bank, Tata Elxsi
summary: Markets traded under pressure amid renewed geopolitical concerns on Tuesday. Several companies like Groww and Infosys will be in focus today. Anand Rathi Share reported a significant profit rise for the June quarter. IDBI Bank clarified reports regarding a potential Fairfax Financial offer. Tata Elxsi announced an 18.2% profit increase supported by strong tech spending.

title: Net direct tax mop-up jumps 16% to Rs 6.5 lakh crore
summary: India's net direct tax collections increased by 16.4 percent to ₹6.51 lakh crore. Corporate tax collections saw a significant 22 percent rise during this period. Non-corporate tax collections grew by 11.7 percent, showing broad-based growth. Refunds issued also increased by 14.6 percent compared to last year. These figures indicate robust revenue growth for the current fiscal year.

title: RBI tightens governance framework, redefines bank board responsibilities
summary: The RBI has revised bank board governance norms, effective October 1.  Boards will now focus on strategy and risk oversight, delegating operational matters.  Specialised committees will handle routine approvals and reviews, enhancing efficiency.  This move aims to allow directors more time for long-term strategic issues.  The changes follow draft proposals and aim for better corporate governance.

title: HCL Tech tumbles 4.5% as analysts cut price targets after Q1 results
summary: HCL Technologies shares dropped significantly on Tuesday following analyst target reductions. Most foreign brokerages trimmed their price targets after the company's first-quarter results. The Nifty IT index also saw a decline, mirroring the broader market trend. Despite this, many domestic brokers maintain a positive outlook on the stock. Analysts still project a potential upside from current trading levels.

title: Middle East tensions push oil above $85, but equities show resilience on Trump's TACO trade
summary: Middle East tensions have pushed oil prices above eighty-five dollars a barrel.  Indian equities declined moderately after recent geopolitical developments.  Investors are betting on Donald Trump's tendency to de-escalate conflicts.  This resilience suggests markets are accustomed to regional geopolitical tensions.  Higher oil prices are negative for India, but investors expect rewards.

title: RBI governor meets bank CEOs to discuss AI, geopolitics and ECL guidelines
summary: Central bank governor Sanjay Malhotra met bank chief executives on Tuesday. Geopolitical complexities and artificial intelligence were key discussion points. New expected credit loss guidelines also featured in the important talks. Some banks shared their FCNR(B) scheme inflow estimates with the governor. Oil prices have spiked over nine percent after recent military actions.

title: Each of Vedanta’s five demerged entities can become worth $100 bn: Anil Agarwal
summary: Vedanta's chairman sees five demerged entities reaching $100 billion valuations. The company plans significant production increases across aluminium, oil, and steel. Expansion targets are set for power generation and nuclear energy ventures. Vedanta also aims to boost zinc, lead, silver, and copper output significantly.

title: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: Check how Indian stock market is expected to trade on 15 July
summary: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: The trends on Gift Nifty also indicate a muted start for the Indian benchmark index. The Gift Nifty was trading around 24,032 level, a premium of nearly 8 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close.

title: From Gift Nifty, US inflation, IBM shares to Kospi’s rally: 8 key things that changed for Indian stock market overnight
summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 24,049 level, a premium of nearly 25 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a mildly positive start for the Indian stock market indices.

title: Crude oil prices extend gains amid rising US-Iran war tensions; Brent crude surges past $85 per barrel
summary: US-Iran war: Brent crude was up $1.46, or 1.72%, at $86.19 per barrel, while WTI gained $1.11, or 1.4%, to trade at $80.40 per barrel.

summary: Not all stock indices become popular benchmarks for investment. Could niche indices launched by stock exchanges, with their unique focus, hold your next investment strategy?&nbsp;

title: Hero MotoCorp's EV investing strategy meets its first setback
summary: The two-wheeler maker has marked down the value of its Zero Motorcycles investment by 92% even as its technology partnership continues, highlighting the contrasting fortunes of its electric vehicle startup bets.

title: Why Indian homes are changing IKEA's global playbook
summary: IKEA is investing over  ₹20,000 crore in India, adapting its strategy to reflect local consumer preferences for living rooms and multifunctional spaces, unlike in Europe. The retailer plans to open smaller city stores and expand local sourcing to meet evolving demands and enhance affordability.

title: AI hiring shifts to value maxing from token maxing
summary: The AI hiring playbook is changing. Recruiters are moving beyond prompt counts and token usage, seeking candidates who can demonstrate measurable business outcomes as companies shift from AI experimentation to cost-conscious deployment and ROI-driven hiring.

title: Sprng, a new thread in Grasim's growth story as Birla's flagship
summary: The Sprng Energy deal highlights Grasim's transformation from a manufacturing company into the Aditya Birla Group's flagship incubation platform. Like the flagships of the Adani group and Reliance Industries, it is now becoming the engine for the group's next phase of growth.
